compare.heroSubtitle: Pick a left and right file. We parse both in your browser (up to {mb} MB each, max {maxRows} rows loaded per file). compare.dropHint: Drop a CSV here or browse
compare.needBoth: Load both CSV files to see the comparison and read-only grids.
compare.summaryColumnsReordered: Columns share the same fields; the right file was reordered to match the left for comparison.
compare.summaryStructureMismatch: Column headers or fields differ, so automatic cell-by-cell diff is disabled. Use the grids for a visual comparison.
compare.summaryIdentical: No differing cells in overlapping rows.
compare.optMatchColumnsByName: Match columns by header (same fields, different order)
compare.warnMatchColumnsOff: Turn this on to compare cell-by-cell when only column order differs.
compare.optAlignByKey: Match rows by a key column (instead of line number)
compare.exportDiffUnavailable: Enable a valid column layout to export a diff report.

legal.privacyDescription: How {name} handles data and privacy when you use the site and in-browser utilities.
legal.privacyNoticeBody: {name} includes many utilities, such as data viewers and converters, PDF, image, and video tools, developer and color helpers, and related pages. Features that run in your browser are built so your files and inputs stay on your device and are not uploaded to our servers for that processing unless a specific tool clearly says otherwise. We may still collect ordinary technical and analytics data when you load pages. Some deployments may show ads and use cookies for measurement and fraud prevention.
legal.termsDescription: Terms of use for {name} across all pages and utilities.
legal.termsBody: These terms apply to your entire use of {name}, including every page and in-browser utility we offer (for example data, PDF, image, video, developer, and color tools). By using the site, you agree to them.
legal.termsNoticeBody: {name} is provided “as is”. You must not use the service for unlawful purposes or to process data you are not allowed to handle. You are responsible for outputs you create and how you use them. We may set limits to keep the service reliable and fair.
legal.termsNoticeHint: If you operate this deployment, update these terms with your legal entity details and any additional disclosures required in your region.

marketing.privacyBody: Many utilities run in your browser: files and inputs usually stay on this device and are not uploaded to our servers for that processing. Some tools may save progress in local storage on this device. Other pages may use standard analytics. Read the Privacy Policy below for full details.

imageConvertPair.subtitle: Convert {from} to {to} locally in your browser. No uploads. Output defaults to {to}.
imageConvertPair.intro1: People search for {from} to {to} when they need a smaller file, a format their CMS accepts, or a pixel layout editors can open without extra plugins. This page runs the same Localtool image converter as the main hub, preset to {to} output.
imageConvertPair.intro2: Decoding and encoding happen in your browser tab. Nothing is uploaded to our servers for conversion. Use fast mode for PNG, JPG, WebP, and AVIF when your browser supports it, or enable the advanced path for GIF, BMP, TIFF, ICO, HEIC, or traced SVG when needed.
imageConvertPair.faq1Question: How do I convert {from} to {to} without uploading my files?
imageConvertPair.faq1Answer: Open this page, add images from your device or a CORS-enabled direct link, keep output on {to}, then download. Processing stays in your browser; we do not receive your image bytes for conversion.
imageConvertPair.faq2Answer: Lossy formats (such as JPG, WebP, or AVIF) use a quality slider. Lossless formats (such as PNG or SVG from tracing) avoid photo compression but may produce larger files. Pick the format that matches your next step, not only the smallest size.
imageConvertPair.faq3Question: Is {from} to {to} the same as the main image converter?
imageConvertPair.faq3Answer: Yes. The hub at /image-convert lists the full tool; this URL sets the default output to {to} and targets people who already know their source is {from}. You can still change output format in the tool if you need a different export.
imageConvertPair.keywordsLine: {from} to {to} | convert {from} to {to} online free | {from} to {to} converter | free {from} to {to} no upload | browser {from} to {to} private | local {from} to {to}

gradients.faq1Question: Can I lock a stop and regenerate the gradient?
gradients.faq1Answer: Yes. Click the lock icon on any stop to keep it fixed while you change harmony mode, base color, or sliders.
gradients.faq2Question: How does contrast guidance work here?
gradients.faq2Answer: For each stop we compute the best text color (black/white) using WCAG contrast ratio, and show an AA/AAA badge.
gradients.faq3Answer: Copy CSS linear-gradient, and download PNG, JSON, and a CSS file containing variables plus the full gradient.

gradientsBest.faq3Answer: Yes. Harmony modes, locks, sliders, and exports work the same as when you build a gradient from scratch; the preset is only a starting point.
